data.title: Wall Street advances for a second day as investors hope for a U.S.-Iran peace deal
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were comfortably in the green on Tuesday as investors grew optimistic about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks that could ease tensions in the Middle East.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.8%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.5%.

The gains followed comments from President Donald Trump indicating that Iran had reached out for potential negotiations, even as the U.S. moved forward with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

Asian equities also rallied on hopes that a deal could lower oil prices and support global growth.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point lower at 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was unchanged at 3.78%.

On the economic front, the March U.S. Producer Price Index came in at +0.5% MoM vs. +1.2% consensus and +0.5% prior (revised from +0.7%), and +4.0% YoY vs. +4.7% consensus and +3.4% prior.

Core PPI, which excludes foods and energy, was +0.1% MoM vs. +0.5% consensus and +0.3% prior (revised from +0.5%), and +3.8% YoY vs. +4.2% consensus and +3.8% prior (revised from +3.9%).

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $93.9, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $96.4 per barrel.

data.title: Samsung hikes prices on smartphones and tablets as Apple holds steady
data.description:
Samsung (SSNLF) has recently hiked up the price on several of its smartphones and tablets in the U.S. market, while Apple's latest iPhone 17 models have held steady.

Samsung's Galaxy Flip 7 with 512GB of storage has increased to $1,299 from $1,219 in the U.S. over the past week, in a move observed by PhoneArena. The Galaxy S25 FE 256GB rose to $749 from $709, and the Galaxy S25 Edge 512GB climbed to $1,299 from $1,219.

The Galaxy Z Fold 7 1TB also increased to $2,499 from $2,419. However, its entry-level 256G remained at $1,999.

The price hikes are likely linked to rising memory costs as the AI data center buildout continues to take precedence when it comes to supply.

In contrast, Apple has not increased the sticker price on its iPhone 17 lineup. The iPhone 17 Pro's starting price remains unchanged at $1,099. The iPhone 17 Air remains at $999, and the iPhone 17 base model still starts at $799. Apple also introduced the iPhone 17e that starts at $599. 

Samsung also edged up its prices on an array of its tablets. The Galaxy Tab S11, S11 Ultra, S10 FE, S10 Lite, S10 Plus, and A11 Plus have all increased in price by at least $50. The S11 Ultra featured the most prominent increases, with the 1TB going to $1,899 from $1,619.

Apple might increase the price of its upcoming iPhone 18 models due in part to the introduction of more powerful chips. The iPhone 17 Pro was priced $100 more than the iPhone 16 Pro. However, the iPhone 17 base model remained the same as the iPhone 16 base model when it was released.

data.title: Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg moves desk to AI lab, sharpening focus: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms' (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg has moved his desk to the AI lab and is coding all day, Semafor reported, citing the company's President and Vice Chairman Dina McCormick.

Zuckerberg has “actually moved his desk and is seated in the AI lab … and he’s coding all day long,” McCormick told Semafor World Economy on Tuesday, the report added.

"I think he feels so strongly that he has to understand it at that level to really think about how to make our model the strongest it can be,"  said McCormick, who joined Meta in January.

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

McCormick also said that she had to transition from the pace of her former roles — ranging from Goldman Sachs to Deputy National Security Advisor for Strategy under President Donald Trump — to the tech giant's breakneck speed, the report noted.

"Silicon Valley, or at least I’ve seen Meta move much, much more quickly than when I worked in government or even on Wall Street," said McCormick.

When asked about the recent rulings on Meta’s legal liability for harm to teen mental health, McCormick noted that, "We disagree respectfully with those verdicts, and we’re appealing."

McCormick added that "I have seen how seriously the leadership of the company takes this to ensure that there is not harmful content, and most importantly, to ensure that parents are empowered."

Last week, Meta unveiled Muse Spark, the first AI model developed by the company's Superintelligence team. Downloads of Meta's AI app on iOS in the U.S. saw an increase of 87% day-over-day on April 8, when the company released Muse Spark, according to market intelligence firm Sensor Tower.

data.title: FCC will have “minor” role in Paramount/Warner Bros. merger, looking into NFL deals, The View
data.description:
The Federal Communications Commission expects to have “a minor, if any, role” in Paramount Skydance’s (PSKY) acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and will likely only look into the foreign involvement in the deal.

In an interview on CNBC, FCC Commissioner Brendan Carr said that although the deal involves the ownership of CBS, the agency will not be very involved in the regulatory process. While there are some issues regarding the investment of foreign debt, that will likely be the only role the FCC will take.

During the interview, Carr disclosed that his agency has opened an investigation into Disney’s (DIS) “The View” and any violations of FCC rules pertaining to conflicts of interest and allowing equal time for candidates on broadcast TV.

“We will enforce ‘equal time’ rules,” Carr stressed.

“This is more about disclosure…allowing for more speech, not less,” Carr said, adding that if “broadcasters don’t want to comply with their public interest obligation, they can switch to other mediums like streaming and cable.”

When asked about the FCC's stance on partnerships between the National Football League and streaming channels, Carr acknowledged his agency is investigating these deals as they pertain to the “health of broadcasters.”

“The NFL has reached a tipping point where they are putting too many games behind a paywall,” encouraging the FCC to assist the Department of Justice in their antitrust investigation of the league, he noted.

data.title: CME adds Eris SOFR swap options for managing US dollar interest rate risk
data.description:
CME Group (CME) will launch options on Eris SOFR swap futures on June 16, 2026, providing investors with a way to manage U.S. dollar interest rate risk more precisely, the company said Tuesday. The introduction is subject to pending regulatory review.

"Eris SOFR swap options will complement our existing interest rate product suite, which delivers deep liquidity, price transparency, and unmatched margin efficiencies to global market participants," said Agha Mirza, CME Group global head of Rates and OTC Products.

Eris SOFR Swap futures replicate interest rate swap cash flows, offering the standardization and capital savings of exchange-traded instruments, CME said. Adding options on two-year, five-year, and 10-year Eris SOFR Swap futures will support more sophisticated hedging strategies, such as managing non-linear risk in mortgage-backed portfolios, it added.

Michael Riddle, CEO of Eris Innovations, commented, "By mirroring the structure of forward-premium OTC swaptions, Eris SOFR Swap options can deliver cost optimization, margin efficiencies, and trading simplicity amid shifting economic conditions."

The new options add to CME Group's (CME) interest rate market offerings, spanning futures and options for a broad range of benchmark products, including U.S. Treasuries, SOFR, Fed Funds,TBAs, and credit, among others. Its U.S. Treasury and SOFR contracts trade side-by-side on the CME Globex platform with BrokerTec cash securities.

data.title: Oil tanker linked to China made U-turn as ships test U.S. naval blockade of Iran - report
data.description:
A U.S.-sanctioned tanker linked to China that was being watched as one of the first vessels to test President Trump's naval blockade apparently has U-turned after sailing out of the Strait of Hormuz and into the Gulf of Oman, Bloomberg reported Tuesday.

The vessel Rich Starry was blacklisted by the U.S. in 2023 for helping Iran evade energy sanctions and has a history of spoofing its satellite signals, according to TankerTrackers.com, which monitors vessels using satellite data.

Another tanker, the Elpis, was in the strait and headed into the Gulf of Oman just as the blockade began; ship-tracking platforms Kpler and Vortexa indicate the vessel docked at an Iranian port in the gulf before attempting to pass through Hormuz, meaning it could be a potential target for the U.S. naval patrol.

The Elpis paused its journey at roughly the same location that Rich Starry turned around, although it has not broadcast a signal for several hours, meaning it could have carried on its journey or turned back.

China called the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports a "dangerous and irresponsible" move.

Benchmark crude oil futures fell sharply on Tuesday as signs of ​a possible resumption in talks to end the Iran war eased supply concerns stemming ‌from the blockade.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery down 5.9% to $93.23/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery down 4.4% to $88.86/bbl.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

data.title: Worksport CEO buys 88.2K shares, eyes $35–42M revenue in 2026
data.description:
• Worksport (WKSP) on Tuesday announced that CEO Steven Rossi bought 88.2K shares at $0.85, worth ~$75K, by converting his pending cash pay into stock—showing strong confidence in the company.
  • The buy comes amid rapid growth, with revenue increasing from ~$1.5M (2023) to $16.1M (2025) and aiming for $35–$42M in 2026.
  • Margins improved from ~11% to ~30%, targeting ~35% ahead in 2026.
  • Expansion is strong, as the dealer network expanded to 550+ locations, with ongoing product launches.
  • The company expects to turn cash-flow positive in 2026 at ~$9–$11M quarterly revenue.
  • Worksport (WKSP) is trading around ~$0.99, up ~4.7% today.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ex-NATO chief warns U.K. is ‘under-prepared’ as defense spending row intensifies
data.description:
A senior figure in the U.K.’s Labor Party and former head of NATO has publicly rebuked Prime Minister Keir Starmer, arguing the government is not committing sufficient resources to national defense and is exposing the country to heightened risk, Reuters reported Tuesday.

George Robertson, who served as defense secretary in the late 1990s before leading NATO, said in remarks there is a disconnect between the prime minister’s language on security and actual spending decisions. He contended that the government has so far been unwilling to make the level of investment required.

Downing Street pushed back strongly. A spokesperson for the prime minister rejected the criticism outright, saying the government remains focused on ensuring that defense spending aligns with current and emerging threats.

The criticism also comes amid heightened scrutiny of Britain’s military posture overseas, following reports that a drone linked to Iran last month struck a U.K. airbase on Cyprus during a period of escalating hostilities between the United States and Israel and Iranian forces. The incident fueled concerns among defense observers about force protection and preparedness.

Robertson, who contributed to a Strategic Defense Review commissioned after Labor’s return to power in 2024, is expected to sharpen his critique in a speech later in Salisbury. Excerpts indicate he will argue that the U.K. has become increasingly vulnerable to external dangers.

In those prepared remarks, he is also set to single out Chancellor Rachel Reeves for giving minimal attention to defense in recent fiscal statements, including failing to address the issue in a subsequent update.

According to the excerpts, Robertson plans to warn that Britain is inadequately prepared for current threats, describing the country as exposed and insufficiently protected.

The government hasn’t yet released a long-term defense investment framework originally expected late last year, intended to implement recommendations from the 2024 review. That report emphasized the need to pivot toward emerging capabilities such as drone systems, cyber operations and data-driven warfare, drawing lessons from the conflict in Ukraine.

Starmer has argued that years of underfunding under previous Conservative governments created current shortfalls, and has pledged a significant increase in defense spending, targeting 3% of GDP in the next parliamentary term.

Still, Robertson is expected to accuse Britain’s leadership of complacency, criticizing what he characterizes as misguided fiscal decisions made by officials outside the defense establishment. He is also set to argue that rising welfare expenditures risk crowding out essential military investment.

The comments come as the government frames a more unstable global environment, highlighted by escalating tensions involving Iran, as justification for strengthening both economic resilience and military readiness.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Only two mid cap communication stocks have A grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the communication services sector enters reporting season navigating a complex mix of tailwinds and headwinds, making upward EPS revisions a particularly valuable signal of where analyst conviction is building.

Within the mid-cap communication services space, that signal is notably rare: only two stocks have earned an "A" EPS revision grade from Seeking Alpha, reflecting the selective nature of analyst optimism in this segment of the market.

Array Digital Infrastructure (AD), a wireless telecommunications provider that has beaten earnings estimates in three of the last four quarters and Sphere Entertainment (SPHR), which delivered a stunning Q4 EPS turnaround. They stand alone as the mid-cap communication services names where analysts have been meaningfully revising their earnings expectations higher ahead of Q1 reports.

  • Array Digital Infrastructure, Inc. (AD) — EPS Revision Grade: A
  • Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR) — EPS Revision Grade: A

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)

data.title: Dollar share falls to 45%—are Bitcoin, gold the silent reserve rivals?
data.description:
The US dollar is still the world’s dominant reserve currency—but its supremacy is slowly weakening. And in that gap, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is starting to act less like a risky asset and more like a global backup plan.

According to Global Markets Investor, the  dollar’s share of global FX and gold reserves has fallen to ~45%, down from over ~60% at the start of the decade. IMF data also shows that, excluding gold (XAUUSD:CUR), the dollar now makes up ~57% of global reserves—the lowest level since 1994.

The direction of change is clear: central banks are slowly reducing overreliance on a single reserve asset. Three major reasons behind this shift are inflation volatility, shifting interest rate regimes, and rising geopolitical tensions.

Reportedly, global trade settled in dollars has dropped to ~40% from ~54% in just a few years, especially after the 2022 freezing of Russian reserves.

What is unfolding here is not just "de-dollarization," but instead a broader search for alternative stores of value. Among traditional assets, gold has emerged as the primary beneficiary of this diversification trend.

Central banks now hold more gold (XAUUSD:CUR) than US Treasuries (value-adjusted) for the first time, signaling a clear move toward politically neutral reserve assets, reported The Economic Times. However, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) is no longer the only option being considered.

Most prominent companies have already started treating Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a reserve asset. For example, Euronext-listed Capital B has increased its holdings to 2,925 BTC, showing a growing corporate view of BTC as a long-term treasury reserve asset.

Even entities like MicroStrategy (MSTR) continue to increase Bitcoin (BTC-USD) exposure. At the same time, the Bitcoin Policy Institute recently suggested that countries like Taiwan could use Bitcoin (BTC-USD) as a reserve asset to reduce dependence on the US dollar and protect against geopolitical risks.

What makes this more interesting is Bitcoin’s recent performance. Even during 46 days of US-Iran geopolitical tension, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has stayed strong.

It is currently trading near ~$75K, up around 10% in the past week and about 5% in the past month, despite continuous extreme fear sentiment across investors.

In conclusion, the US dollar is losing some of its dominance, but it’s not being replaced. Instead, the global money system is moving into a “coexistence era,” where multiple reserve assets exist together, each playing a role in providing stability, trust, and liquidity.
